evaluationCriteria.detail: lfm25-1p2b-thinking-local qualified for website_audit.lighthouse_handoff / priority_helper with score 0.9167. Benchmark Lab evidence: 11/12 PASS.
Qualification evidence approved-with-limitations by JP Pannell at benchmark-lab/evidence/reviews/lfm25-1p2b-thinking-lighthouse-priority-v1-review.json
34 actionable shadow comparisons collected (37 total minus 3 non-actionable recommendation-unavailable)
3 input variants (lh-shadow-001, lh-shadow-007, lh-shadow-010) each run 3 times = 9 total consistency trials
Shadow router correctly identifies qualified capability and produces disagree comparisons for all actionable records
34/37 records have actionable comparisons (91.89%). 3 non-actionable from pre-qualification era
Policy store healthy, enforcementLocked=false, loadError=null after schema fix

No human-reviewed paired comparison of current vs qualified model outputs has been performed. Shadow records validate routing recommendation only — they do not establish output quality of the qualified model for these specific inputs.
Track not yet approved for enforcement. Requires explicit approval via POST /enforcement/approve before advancing from shadow to eligible state
Ollama runtime available on 127.0.0.1:11434 with all required models

limitations: Shadow records validate routing recommendation only — all 34 actionable records are 'disagree' because the current model (llama3.2) differs from the qualified capability (lfm25-1p2b-thinking-local). These records do not contain execution output from the qualified model.
No human has reviewed the paired outputs. The 'needs-human-review' verdict means the evidence supports shadow routing integration but does not yet support pilot activation.

The qualified model's runtime model name ('hf.co/LiquidAI/LFM2.5-1.2B-Thinking-GGUF:latest') differs from its capability manifest id ('lfm25-1p2b-thinking-local'). The shadow router uses manifest ids, so the current model and recommended capability never compare as 'agree'.

Slice 1 qualification evidence (benchmark-lab/ evidence) provides the 11/12 PASS evaluation of the qualified model against a 12-scenario suite. This is the primary output-quality evidence for the model — the shadow records do not duplicate this.
No enforcement decisions have been applied yet. All evidence is purely observational (shadow mode).
Agreement rate is 0%. This is expected and correct — the router identifies a different model as preferred.
pilotRecommendation: Do not activate pilot yet. Shadow router integration is validated (34 actionable disagree records, 91.89% coverage). Slice 1 qualification evidence exists (11/12 PASS, score 0.9167). But no human-reviewed paired output comparison has been performed for specific Track inputs. Required before pilot: (1) Run paired current-vs-qualified model evaluations on representative cases, (2) Human review of paired outputs with per-disagreement records, (3) Approve track and set state to eligible after review passes.
requiredActions: Run paired evaluations: same Track input with current model (llama3.2) and qualified model (lfm25-1p2b-thinking-local)
Human review of paired outputs: record per-case preference, grounding problems, schema validity
Produce per-disagreement review records with record ID, case ID, preferred result, and pilot-blocking issues
After human review passes: POST /enforcement/approve, POST /enforcement/set to eligible
Document policy migration for legacy expiresAt on overrides (see docs/06-decisions/decision-log.md)
